comment_text.sn14.14:1.2: Here “element” means something like a “personality trait”.
comment_text.sn14.14:1.3: Adhimuttika appears to be a Buddhist coinage. The key text is the Divine Net Discourse DN 1, where the Buddha’s ability to discern the different “convictions” of beings is illustrated with the examples of Suppiya who criticized the Buddha, Dhamma, and Saṅgha, while his student Brahmadatta praised them. This speaks to a difference in faith, a quality closely associated with adhimutta (eg. AN 1.208:1.1). DN 1 goes on to discuss the various views, using the unique term adhimuttipadāni (“hypotheses”, “statements of conviction”), which clearly draws on adhimutti in the introduction.

root_text.sn14.14:2.1: Atītampi kho, bhikkhave, addhānaṁ dhātusova sattā saṁsandiṁsu samiṁsu.
root_text.sn14.14:3.1: Anāgatampi kho, bhikkhave, addhānaṁ dhātusova sattā saṁsandissanti samessanti.
root_text.sn14.14:4.1: Etarahipi kho, bhikkhave, paccuppannaṁ addhānaṁ dhātusova sattā saṁsandanti samenti.

translation_text.sn14.14:1.2: “Mendicants, sentient beings join together and converge because of an element.
translation_text.sn14.14:1.3: Those who have inferior convictions join together and converge with those who have inferior convictions.
translation_text.sn14.14:1.4: Those who have good convictions join together and converge with those who have good convictions.
translation_text.sn14.14:2.1: In the past, too, sentient beings came together and converged because of an element. …
translation_text.sn14.14:3.1: In the future, too, sentient beings will join together and converge because of an element. …
translation_text.sn14.14:4.1: At present, too, sentient beings join together and converge because of an element.
translation_text.sn14.14:4.2: Those who have inferior convictions join together and converge with those who have inferior convictions.
translation_text.sn14.14:4.3: Those who have good convictions join together and converge with those who have good convictions.”

variant_text.sn14.14:2.1: kho → khosaddo bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed potthakesu
variant_text.sn14.14:3.1: kho → khosaddo bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed potthakesu
variant_text.sn14.14:4.1: kho → khosaddo bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed potthakesu
